title: Sangameswarar Temple
tagline: Sangameswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Sangameswarar temple (also called Thirunana and Thirukooduthurai) is a temple in Bhavani, in the Erode district, of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.
overview.description: Sangameswarar temple (also called Thirunana and Thirukooduthurai) is a temple in Bhavani, in the Erode district, of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is 15 km from Erode, 30 km from Gobichettipalayam, 56 km from Salem and 106 km from Coimbatore. The temple was built at the confluence of rivers Kaveri, Bhavani and the mystic Amutha (Agaya Gangai).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Bhavani River
topPlaces.description: Bhavani is an Indian river which flows through the states of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. It originates in the Nilgiris in the Western Ghats in Tamil Nadu, and flows westwards into Kerala for some distance, before turning east, and flowing back towards Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kalingarayan Anicut
topPlaces.description: Kalingarayan Anicut is a barrage on the Bhavani River in Erode district, Tamil Nadu. It is located before Kooduthurai where Bhavani meets with Kaveri in Bhavani, Erode.

topPlaces.name: Bhavani, Tamil Nadu
topPlaces.description: Bhavani is a municipality in Erode District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located at the northern periphery of Erode City Municipal Corporation and is around 105 km (65 mi) from Coimbatore and 60 km (37 mi) from Tiruppur and Salem.

topPlaces.name: Bhavani taluk
topPlaces.description: Bhavani taluk was a taluk of Erode district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Bhavani.

topPlaces.name: Mettunasuvanpalayam
topPlaces.description: Mettunasuvanpalayam is a census town in Bhavani taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.

topPlaces.name: Perumalmalai, Erode
topPlaces.description: Perumalmalai is a hillock and neighborhood in Erode.

topPlaces.name: Suriyampalayam
topPlaces.description: Suriyampalayam is a panchayat town in the city of Erode in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the Headquarters for one of the four zones of Erode Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Komarapalayam
topPlaces.description: Komarapalayam is a municipality and textile town situated on the banks of the Kaveri and Bhavani rivers, in Namakkal District, Tamil Nadu, India. Komarapalayam is also called Kumarapalayam.

items.note: Bhavani is an Indian river which flows through the states of Kerala and Tamil Nadu. It originates in the Nilgiris in the Western Ghats in Tamil Nadu, and flows westwards into Kerala for some distance, before turning east, and flowing back towards Tamil Nadu.
Kalingarayan Anicut is a barrage on the Bhavani River in Erode district, Tamil Nadu. It is located before Kooduthurai where Bhavani meets with Kaveri in Bhavani, Erode.

Bhavani is a municipality in Erode District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located at the northern periphery of Erode City Municipal Corporation and is around 105 km (65 mi) from Coimbatore and 60 km (37 mi) from Tiruppur and Salem.
Bhavani taluk was a taluk of Erode district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Bhavani.
Mettunasuvanpalayam is a census town in Bhavani taluk of Erode district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located in the north-western part of the state.
Perumalmalai is a hillock and neighborhood in Erode.
Suriyampalayam is a panchayat town in the city of Erode in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is the Headquarters for one of the four zones of Erode Municipal Corporation.
Komarapalayam is a municipality and textile town situated on the banks of the Kaveri and Bhavani rivers, in Namakkal District, Tamil Nadu, India. Komarapalayam is also called Kumarapalayam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sangameswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sangameswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sangameswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (83 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (92 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sangameswarar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sangameswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sangameswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sangameswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sangameswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bhavani River, Kalingarayan Anicut, Bhavani, Tamil Nadu, Bhavani taluk, Mettunasuvanpalayam.
